items.title: Marine Le Pen: Presidential bid in doubt as court upholds fraud conviction
items.description: French far-right leader Marine Le Pen saw her conviction for fraud upheld by a Paris appeals court on Tuesday. The court sentenced Le Pen to a three-year jail term, but with two years suspended and one year with an electronic ankle tag. It means Le Pen will be eligible to run in France’s 2027 presidential elections but while wearing an electronic bracelet, something she has said is a no-go.

items.title: Watch: Why PM Modi’s Indonesia visit matters for India | Explained
items.description: Nearly 40% of India’s maritime trade passes through one of the world’s busiest shipping lanes, the Strait of Malacca. And the country that sits at the gateway to that strategic chokepoint is Indonesia. That’s why Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to Indonesia, his first bilateral visit to the country since 2018, is about much more than diplomacy. It is about securing India’s maritime interests, expanding economic partnerships and strengthening its position in an increasingly competitive Indo-Pacific. So, why does Indonesia matter so much to India?

items.description: Five co-owners of an Adelaide Plains piggery are collectively accused of ill-treating their animals, including allegedly failing to ensure the animals had "adequate and appropriate living conditions".

items.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
items.description: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

items.title: Uganda coffee exports hit by weaker prices
items.description: Uganda's coffee export earnings dropped sharply in May as falling global prices and lower export volumes weighed on the country's top agricultural export. The decline highlights the vulnerability of Africa's largest coffee exporter to swings in international commodity markets.

items.title: The United States turns 250
items.description: Much of the population thinks the United States is heading in the wrong direction, but there is no agreement at all on what would be the right one. Leer más

items.title: Messi returns 'home' to lead Argentina World Cup charge in Miami
items.description: Argentina's skipper returns to the Florida city he now calls home for a last 32 clash against the surprise package of the tournament: Cape Verde. Leer más

items.title: Arrogance as state policy and the end of Manuel Adorni
items.description: Manuel Adorni's resignation, in which he accused the press of "media attacks," comes across as pretty pathetic from a spokesman and Cabinet chief who built his tenure on arrogance and who now stands accused of corruption. Leer más

items.title: Russian strikes kill at least 26 in Kyiv region on eve of NATO summit
items.description: The attack hit just days after another Russian strike killed more than 30 people in the Ukrainian capital

items.title: After 250 years, the 'American dream' is tarnished but alive
items.description: Whatever its means, the "American dream" still offers hope to millions of people who believe the United States is a place where anyone can succeed, no matter the odds. Leer más

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: More stars or stripes?
items.description: Within a decade of the Civil War, the US economy had overtaken Great Britain and the entire British Empire by around 1895 – from that point onward, it was more a question of the US impact on world history than the reverse. Leer más

items.title: UNICEF: More than 300 children killed in Sudan in last six months
items.description: More than 300 children have been killed or injured in the last six months in the war in Sudan, mostly from drone strikes, the UN's children's agency said on Monday.

items.title: Prosecutors argue man accused of killing Charlie Kirk should stand trial
items.description: Utah prosecutors have begun presenting their case against Tyler James Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk, at a week-long preliminary trial.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
items.description: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

items.title: Algeria: record-low turnout shadows ruling party's election victory
items.description: Algeria's ruling National Liberation Front has emerged as the largest party in parliament after legislative elections marked by a record-low voter turnout of just 21 percent. The poll highlighted growing public apathy despite government assurances of a transparent process.

items.title: Maradona death trial: Star's former manager questions conditions of home care
items.description: Matías Morla tells court Diego Maradona would never have accepted the conditions of the Tigre house where star spent his final days, arguing the home as ill-equipped for a medical emergency. Leer más

items.title: Santilli sworn in as Cabinet chief as Milei reshuffles pack
items.description: Diego Santilli formally took over as President Javier Milei's Cabinet chief on Tuesday, completing a transition triggered by Manuel Adorni's resignation amid a corruption investigation. Leer más

items.title: Marine Le Pen branding as 'victim of judicial harassment' would be 'political stretch'
items.description: Marine Le Pen is set to give a crucial interview on French primetime television on July 7 following a sentence handed down by a court of appeal after she was found guilty of systemising the embezzlement of EU funds within her party, the National Rally. Although she has said she would not run with an electronic tag on, she could well change her mind. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent looks at the implications of such a decision.

items.title: Luis Caputo: Argentina has secured funds for 2026 debt payments
items.description: Economy Minister Luis Caputo says Milei government has secured enough funds to meet this year's external debt obligations; Administration open to international markets to refinance future maturities, but not in a rush. Leer más

items.title: Explosions injure 18 in Damascus during Macron's visit
items.description: As reports of the blasts came in, state television said the Syrian president had welcomed Macron at the presidential palace.

items.title: 2 ships are hit in the latest attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, the U.K. military says
items.description: Iran, which has repeatedly declared that only its approved route through the Strait of Hormuz is safe, said one of the vessels came under attack after ignoring warnings.

items.title: Germany news: Breweries tap thirst for alcohol-free beer
items.description: Germany is producing more non-alcoholic beer as consumer tastes change. Meanwhile, a top court is reviewing a Bavarian law that allows police intervention before a concrete threat has emerged. DW has more.

items.title: Queiroz steps down after Ghana's World Cup disappointment
items.description: Carlos Queiroz has resigned as Ghana head coach after the Black Stars' World Cup campaign ended with a 1-0 defeat to Colombia in the last 16. The Portuguese coach said he leaves with pride but urged reforms off the pitch to secure the team's future success.

items.title: Pix of the Day, 7 July 2026
items.description: Pix of the Day, 7 July 2026

items.title: At least 23 people killed in Sri Lankan prison riot
items.description: At least 23 people have been killed and more than 100 injured in Sri Lanka's deadliest prison riot in over five years.

items.title: Why migrants come to Germany for work and then leave again
items.description: Germany has long attracted skilled workers to its labor market. The country has been much less successful at retaining them. So what is Germany doing wrong?

items.title: Remote NT MLA admits to claiming travel allowance during stays at city home
items.description: Manuel Brown's dumping from the role comes after weeks of scrutiny over his driving record and confirmation he allowed two of his underage children to drive his personal car on an outstation.

items.title: Nigeria to investigate tech firms over news content use
Le Pen sentenced to 15-month ban from public office in EU embezzlement case
items.description: A French ​appeals court on ​July 7 upheld Marine Le Pen's conviction for misusing EU funds ​but shortened ‌her ban ⁠on running for elected office. In theory, this potentially re-opens a path for the ⁠far-right leader to run in the 2027 presidential ​race. But Le Pen is also sentenced to wearing an electronic tag for a year. She had said herself she would not run if that were the case. FRANCE 24's Catherine Norris-Trent tells us the latest from the court.

items.title: Argentina survive Cape Verde scare to reach World Cup last 16
items.description: Cristian Romero's header in injury time secures 3-2 victory over Cape Verde in extra time; African side impressed in hard-fought contest in Miami, but Argentina's resilience and never-say-die attitude saw them through. Leer más

items.title: Financial services AI dangers highlighted by UK regulator's review
items.description: Regulators globally have begun to focus more keenly ‌on the impact of AI, from cyber and operational risks associated with frontier AI models such as Anthropic’s Mythos to the challenges posed by the agentic systems

items.title: Britain's Farage resigns as MP to stand for re-election amid furore over gift worth millions
items.description: Nigel ​Farage, ​the leader of populist party ‌Reform ⁠UK, ‌said on Tuesday ⁠he was resigning as an MP to stand for re-election so that the public could decide whether he should be ousted from parliament over a scandal involving the alleged non-disclosure of a £5 million gift.

items.title: United States at 250: More prosperous, more diverse, yet more divided
items.description: Demographic change and economic growth have reshaped America over the past 50 years; Immigrants remain the strongest believers in American Dream. Leer más

items.title: Egypt coach makes impassioned plea for Palestine in pre-match conference
items.description: Egypt coach makes impassioned plea for Palestine in pre-match conference

items.title: Jailers and officials at Russia's 'torture prisons' in Ukraine exposed by BBC
items.description: Former prisoners accuse the men of abuse in detention centres and want to see them brought to trial.

items.title: Tanzania arrests dozens as security tightens ahead of planned protests
items.description: Tanzanian authorities have arrested dozens of people ahead of planned anti-government protests, intensifying a crackdown on dissent as activists demand democratic reforms and the release of jailed opposition leader Tundu Lissu amid heightened security across the country.

items.title: Ghana rejects Ramaphosa visit over xenophobic attacks on Ghanaians
items.description: Ghana has turned down a state visit request from South African President Cyril Ramaphosa, as tensions rise over a wave of xenophobic violence that has targeted Ghanaian nationals living in South Africa.

items.title: Large crowds of mourners attend slain supreme leader's funeral in Tehran
items.description: Three of Ali Khamenei’s sons joined the mass prayers, but not his successor, Mojtaba, who has not been seen in public since he assumed the role.

items.title: ICAC asks former uni chief how he made 'an error of that magnitude'
items.description: An incoming vice-chancellor was providing input into a university review before his consulting firm later secured millions of dollars of related work, the ICAC has heard.

items.title: Argentina seek to shake off 'Messi-dependence' against Mo Salah's Egypt
items.description: Lionel Scaloni's side escaped a major scare against Cape Verde, but their dependence on Lionel Messi remains a growing concern ahead of a last-16 meeting with Mohamed Salah's Egypt. Leer más

items.title: Genneia files for first Argentine IPO in the United States since 2019
items.description: Power producer Genneia SA files for IPO in United States, the first Argentine firm to do so in at least seven years. Leer más

items.title: Milei tries again to revive Argentina’s dormant mortgage market
items.description: Mortgage-backed home sales surged early in Milei’s Presidency only to evaporate as the government stemmed a currency run by raising interest rates above 100%. Leer más

items.title: U.S. voices concern over China's intercontinental range missile test
items.description: The U.S. also urged China to engage in meaningful arms control discussions and notify the intercontinental-range ballistic missile and space launches
